summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a jazzy May ’77 show featuring a soaring The Other One suite and the best Lazy Lightning/Supplication pairing.
review: The tape delivers crystal‑clear soundboard quality with Keith’s keyboards sitting nicely up in the mix, while the band plays a relaxed yet inventive set. Highlights include an otherworldly The Other One that borders on Dark Star territory and a standout Lazy Lightning → Supplication sequence, plus a surprisingly strong reading of Loser. Overall the performance is praised as one of the top shows of 1977.

ai_rating.rationale: Nearly all reviewers give 5‑star praise for both musical performance and audio fidelity; only minor comments about a slightly tired feel, no technical flaws reported.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix with Keith’s keys prominently placed in the front; no dropout or muddiness mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed, jazzy vibe throughout the night with exploratory improvisation; the set feels well‑balanced and showcases the band’s creative peak in May ’77.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo on Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo (MHUT) is highlighted as a treat, and his guitar tone throughout is praised.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned positively as part of the “Bob & Phil” dynamic that fans love, though no specific performance details are given.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards are lauded for being high in the mix and sounding especially vibrant on this recording.
